name: Abe no Seimei
description: Abe no Seimei was a legendary onmyōji, a practitioner of onmyōdō, a traditional Japanese esoteric cosmology. He served as an advisor to emperors and nobles, performing divinations, exorcisms, and other rituals to protect the imperial court and ensure the well-being of the nation. Born in the Heian period, his parentage is shrouded in mystery, with some legends claiming his mother was a fox spirit, Kuzunoha, who imbued him with supernatural abilities. Seimei's mastery of onmyōdō allowed him to manipulate shikigami, spirits he could summon and control to perform various tasks. He is credited with predicting natural disasters, warding off evil spirits, and even manipulating the weather. His most notable achievements include the exorcism of a vengeful spirit that plagued the imperial family and the creation of powerful protective spells. Abe no Seimei's legacy extends far beyond his lifetime, influencing Japanese folklore, literature, and popular culture. He remains a powerful symbol of wisdom, magic, and protection, revered as a guardian against evil and a master of the occult. His image continues to be invoked in modern-day practices and beliefs.
subtitle: Master Onmyōji and Protector of Japan

keyMyths.title: The Fox Spirit Mother
keyMyths.description: Abe no Seimei's mother is often depicted as Kuzunoha, a fox spirit who transformed into a human. This origin explains his innate magical abilities and deep connection to the spiritual realm, granting him extraordinary powers.

keyMyths.title: Controlling Shikigami
keyMyths.description: Seimei could summon and control shikigami, spirits that served as his familiars. He used them for various tasks, including spying, protection, and even combat, showcasing his mastery over the spirit world.

keyMyths.title: Protecting the Emperor
keyMyths.description: Abe no Seimei frequently used his powers to protect the Emperor and the Imperial Court from evil influences. He performed rituals and cast spells to ward off curses and ensure the Emperor's safety, securing the nation's prosperity.

sources.author: Helen Hardacre
sources.description: Provides context on the historical background of Onmyōdō and its relationship to the state.
